name: Dzongkha
notes: Dzongkha, national language of Bhutan, written in the Tibetan script (Uchen/Joyi), an abugida. The grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Tibetan-script orthography is deeply historical: the written stack (prefix, superscript, root, subscript, suffix, post-suffix letters) reflects Old Tibetan and maps to the modern Dzongkha pronunciation only through a large set of stack-level rules producing tone and vowel changes — it is not a letter-by-letter transcription. A correct spec needs the Roman Dzongkha transcription rules of van Driem's grammar; that research has not been done. Metadata only.
